title: Platanthera holochila
intro: Platanthera holochila, also known as the pale yellow-green lip orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Platanthera holochila include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.

This species belongs to the genus Platanthera, which is a group of terrestrial orchids found in various parts of the world.
